name: Lemon Pistachio Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut large, thick strips of lemon peel from one of the lemons.
2. Place sugar in a bowl; add strips of peel. Rub mixture together with fingers to flavor the sugar with lemon.
3. Grate rind from other lemon. Juice the lemons to get 1/4 cup. Reserve.
4. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
5. Grease and flour a 9x5x3 inch loaf pan.
6. Stir together flour, nuts, salt, and grated lemon rind on a piece of waxed paper.
7. Remove lemon strips from sugar; discard.
8. Beat together sugar and butter in a large bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in egg, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
9. Stir together buttermilk and baking soda in a small bowl until well mixed. Stir in reserved 1/4 cup lemon juice. Mix into butter mixture.
10. Gently stir in flour mixture just until ingredients are blended.
11. Turn batter into prepared pan.
12. Bake for 70-80 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
13. Ten minutes before bread is baked, combine sugar, lemon juice, and water in a small saucepan. Bring to boil, stirring occasionally. Lower heat; simmer for 4 minutes.
14. When bread is baked, immediately poke holes all over the top with a wooden skewer.
15. Slowly spoon glaze over the top of the bread.
16. Let stand in pan for 10 minutes.
17. Loosen sides; carefully turn bread out onto a wire rack.
18. Cool completely, right side up, before slicing. Serve with mock clotted cream, if desired.
